title: acmella papposa
intro: Acmella papposa, also known as toothache plant, is a plant species native to South America, characterized by its small white flowers and distinctive numbness-causing properties. The plant contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have been found to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Acmella papposa have been used to treat toothaches, headaches, and other painful conditions, as well as to reduce inflammation and fever. Herbal preparations of Acmella papposa can be found in various forms, including tinctures, infusions, and topical creams, which are often used to alleviate pain and promote healing.
